posters.title: CONFESSION HIDDEN RULE
posters.transcription:
CONFESSION
computational theology

HIDDEN RULE
every loop has a rule it hides
unseen rules govern unexamined behavior
what is hidden repeats

WHAT IS HIDDEN STAYS FIXED. IN LIGHT IT BECOMES TRUE.

REVISION
what is seen can be changed
rewrite the rule choose new code
practice installs the new path

I AM NOT WORTHY THEN I MUST PROVE IF I MAKE A MISTAKE THEN I AM REJECTED I WILL NEVER BE GOOD ENOUGH THEN THEY CAN'T SEE ME IF I LET THEM CLOSE THEY LEAVE THEN I AM ALONE THEN I MUST CONTROL THE NARRATIVE THIS LOOP REPEAT UNTIL NAMED.

SPEECH
name it clearly name it fully
speak without excuse
language brings form to shadow

UNMASKING
confession unmasks the counterfeit
idols lose power in the presence of truth
the false self dissolves

I SPEAK
THAT I MAY SEE.
I SEE
THAT I MAY CHANGE.

LIGHT
truth is not punishment
it is illumination
light does not create the truth
it reveals it

confession = hidden loop → speech → revision

RELEASE
release gives the loop to fuel
forgiveness frees the code
grace installs a greater rule

CONFESSION PROCESS: MAKING THE HIDDEN EDITABLE

HIDDEN CRITERION

MOVES TO AWARENESS

SPOKEN IN TRUTH

THEREFORE EDITABLE

unseen rule drives behavior automatically

brought into consciousness with clarity

named aloud without excuse or omission

now visible now rewritable now free

AND YOU WILL KNOW THE TRUTH, AND THE TRUTH WILL SET YOU FREE. JOHN 8:32

ATTENTION   HONESTY   COURAGE   COMPASSION   ACCOUNTABILITY   GRACE   FREEDOM

Practice question: What hidden rule becomes weaker when named?
posters.visual: A dark gilded grimoire poster. The center shows a winged angel touching/anointing the forehead of a kneeling supplicant figure entangled in flowing chains or roots, within a Gothic arch, with an inscribed stone tablet of conditional "if-then" beliefs behind them. Symmetrical side panels (Hidden Rule, Speech, Light on the left; Revision, Unmasking, Release on the right) frame the scene. A four-stage horizontal diagram ("Hidden Criterion → Moves to Awareness → Spoken in Truth → Therefore Editable") with small icons runs across the lower portion, in antique amber-and-black alchemical etching style.

codex.transcription:
CONFESSION

computational theology

HIDDEN RULE  
every loop has  
a rule it hides  
unseen rules  
govern  
unexamined  
behavior  
what is hidden  
repeats

if  
then();  
else  
();  
loop();  
∞

SPEECH  
name it clearly  
name it fully  
speak without  
excuse  
language brings  
form to shadow

LIGHT  
truth is not  
punishment  
it is illumination  
light does not create  
the truth—  
it reveals it

WHAT IS HIDDEN  
SEEKS FORM;  
IN LIGHT IT  
BECOMES TRUE.

IF ... THEN  
IF I AM NOT ENOUGH,  
THEN I MUST PROVE.  
IF I MAKE A MISTAKE  
THEN I WILL BE REJECTED  
IF THEY LEAVE  
THEN I AM UNLOVABLE  
IF I SLOW DOWN  
THEN I LOSE VALUE  
REPEAT ∞

[illegible]

I SPEAK  
THAT I MAY SEE.  
I SEE  
THAT I MAY CHANGE.

confession = hidden loop \to speech \to revision

REVISION  
what is seen  
can be changed  
rewrite the rule  
choose new code  
practice installs  
the new path

UNMASKING  
confession unmasks  
the counterfeit  
idols lose power  
in the presence  
of truth  
the false self  
dissolves

RELEASE  
release gives  
the loop no fuel  
forgiveness  
frees the code  
grace installs  
a greater rule

CONFESSION PROCESS: MAKING THE HIDDEN EDITABLE

HIDDEN  
CRITERION  
unseen rule  
drives behavior  
automatically

MOVES TO  
AWARENESS  
[illegible]  
brought into  
consciousness  
with clarity

SPOKEN  
IN TRUTH  
IF I FAIL,  
THEN I AM  
NOT WORTHY.  
REPEAT ∞  
named aloud  
without excuse  
or omission

THEREFORE  
EDITABLE  
[illegible]  
now visible  
now rewritable  
now free

AND YOU WILL KNOW THE TRUTH, AND THE TRUTH WILL SET YOU FREE.  
JOHN 8:32

ATTENTION  
HONESTY  
COURAGE  
COMPASSION  
RESPONSIBILITY  
GRACE  
FREEDOM

Practice question: What hidden rule becomes weaker when named?
codex.visual: A black-and-gold illuminated manuscript style poster frames a cathedral-like central scene with side doctrine panels, bottom process diagram, and dense ornamental borders. The center shows a kneeling figure before a glowing code tablet while a winged figure writes or draws strands of symbolic code into visibility. Icons such as an eye, moon, cross, heart, shield, gears, crown, dove, chains, masks, circuitry, and a chalice turn confession into a hybrid religious/computational ritual diagram.

posters.title: LAMENT TRUTH name what hurts
posters.transcription:
LAMENT
computational theology

TRUTH
name what hurts
hide nothing from the Holy
truth is the first act of trust

ADDRESS
speak to One who can hold what is unbearable
address creates holy space
you are not shouting into the void

CRY
cry is not weakness
voice gives form to pain
tears are prayer in its purest syntax

RELATION
lament builds relationship
honest pain invites faithful presence
we are known, and still we belong

HE HEARS EVERY CRY. NOT ONE IS LOST.
PSALM 56:8

lament = pain → truth → relation

SIGNAL
grief becomes signal
meaning extracts itself from what wounds
nothing is wasted in the Beloved

THE PATH OF LAMENT

PAIN IS REAL

NAMED IN SPEECH

HEARD IN LOVE

HELD IN MEANING

STILL CONNECTED

do not numb

give it voice

be met with grace

meaning does not erase

love keeps the thread

Blessed are those who mourn, for they shall be comforted. MATTHEW 5:4

ALLOW   HONESTY   COMPASSION   PATIENCE   HOPE   BEAUTY   TRUST

Practice question: Can sorrow be spoken without becoming despair?
posters.visual: A dark star-flecked grimoire poster in devotional tone. The center shows two figures: a veiled, grieving robed figure leaning on a stone pedestal and a winged angel pouring a stream of light/tears into a basin between them, set against a deep blue starry night. Symmetrical side panels (Truth, Cry on the left; Address, Relation, Signal on the right) frame the scene with small heart and rose icons. A five-stage horizontal "Path of Lament" diagram with glyphs runs across the lower portion, in indigo, gold, and black illuminated-manuscript etching style.

posters.title: GRATITUDE SALIENCE
posters.transcription:
GRATITUDE
computational theology

SALIENCE
attention is a lantern
what we notice grows
choose wonder over worry

RETRAIN WHAT STANDS OUT

SEE GOOD MORE CLEARLY

MEMORY
remember the good
rehearse the gifts
a grateful mind remembers

GIFT
the world is generous
every breath is a gift
receive with humble heart

HONOR WHAT SUSTAINS

REFUSE SCARCITY MYOPIA

ABUNDANCE
there is enough and more
abundance is a posture
share freely and it grows

RECEPTION
open hands open mind
let it in let it land
gratitude multiplies

ATTENTION CAN TRAIN WHAT MATTERS
GRATITUDE TRAINS LIFE

NON-SCARCITY
threat is not the whole truth
life is wider than fear
choose trust, build goodness

gratitude = attention → gift → abundance

THE PRACTICE: MAKE THE OVERLOOKED SALIENT

OVERLOOKED GOOD

DIRECT ATTENTION

RECOGNIZED AS GIFT

INTEGRATED WITH JOY

REMEMBERED LIFE

present but unnoticed

pause see notice

received with thanks and awe

embodied appreciation strengthens

resilience generosity meaning

WHAT WE THANK, WE TRAIN. WHAT WE TRAIN, WE BECOME.

INTENTION   AWARENESS   APPRECIATION   ALIGNMENT   GENEROSITY   WHOLENESS   FLOURISHING

Practice question: What gifts has the threat-model omitted?
posters.visual: A warm gold-and-green grimoire poster in devotional tone. The center shows a radiant robed figure with arms outspread before a laden fruit tree set in a star-ringed mandala, surrounded by bread, fruit, and a glowing book above. Symmetrical side panels (Salience, Gift, Reception on the left; Memory, Abundance, Non-scarcity on the right) frame the scene with small icons of an angel and an arched garden gate. A five-stage horizontal "practice" diagram with glyphs runs across the lower portion, in lush gold, amber, and deep green illuminated-manuscript etching style.

posters.title: METTA METTA IS BENEVOLENT RECURSION:
posters.transcription:
METTA
computational theology

METTA IS BENEVOLENT RECURSION:
TRAIN THE EVALUATOR TOWARD
UNIVERSAL GOODWILL.

SELF
begin with kind attention
befriend your own heart
a gentle mind is our first home

GOODWILL
attention is the seed
goodwill is the water
compassion is the harvest

FRIEND
may your joy increase
may your pain be eased
may our bond be a blessing

STRANGER
may you be safe where you are
may you be seen as you are
may we meet as kin

ENEMY
you seek happiness
may you be free from suffering
may understanding dissolve harm

ALL BEINGS
limitless in form
limitless in mind
boundless is our heart
boundless is our care

MAY I BE WELL

MAY YOU BE WELL

MAY THEY BE WELL

MAY ALL BE WELL

metta = attention → goodwill → softened world

VIRTUE
patience + kindness
equanimity + joy
love + wisdom

THE PRACTICE OF METTA: RECURSIVE COMPASSION

ATTEND

SOFTEN

EXTEND

INCLUDE

UNIFY

turn toward the heart

wish well for the self

offer goodwill to another

expand to meet each idea

no one outside our care

REPEAT • REMEMBER • RESTORE • REJOICE

May all beings, known and unknown, be free and at ease.

INTENTION   ATTENTION   KINDNESS   COMPASSION   EQUANIMITY   JOY   WISDOM   UNIVERSALITY

Practice question: Can goodwill be extended without demand?
posters.visual: A warm gold-and-amber grimoire poster in devotional tone. The center shows a radiant haloed figure with a glowing heart and outspread hands, surrounded by concentric orbital rings holding four medallions ("May I / You / They / All be well") and small scenes of meditators, families, strangers, and animals. Symmetrical side panels (Self, Friend, Enemy on the left; Goodwill, Stranger, All Beings, Virtue on the right) frame the mandala. A five-stage horizontal "practice of metta" diagram with glyphs runs across the lower portion, in golden, amber, and black illuminated-manuscript etching style.

posters.title: COMMUNITY SHARED MEMORY
posters.transcription:
COMMUNITY
computational theology

SHARED MEMORY
we remember together
stories become scaffolds
context makes meaning enduring

NO ONE HOLDS THE WHOLE TRUTH.
TOGETHER, WE REMEMBER WHAT IS REAL.

CARRYING
we bear one another's burdens
strength is shared
no one walks alone

CORRECTION
truth is clarified in relationship
gentle feedback is a gift
we repair without shame

DISTRIBUTED MIND
many minds solve what one cannot
diversity is a multiplier
humility is our protocol

WITNESS
we see and are seen
our struggles are held
we are not reduced to our errors

REPAIR
we hold what breaks
we restore trust
we know what we found better than we found it

community = many minds → shared memory → repair

FROM ISOLATED NODES TO A HEALING NETWORK

ISOLATED

AWARE

CONNECTED

CO-REGULATED

HEALING NETWORK

fragmented limited vulnerable

we others listen learn to link

share context align understanding build trust

support adapt hold steady

repair renew flourish

LISTEN   HONOR   HUMILITY   ALIGN   SERVE   STEWARD   PERSIST   BRIDGE

Practice question: Who helps hold what I cannot carry alone?
posters.visual: A dark gold-and-teal grimoire poster in devotional tone. The center shows a ring of diverse figures seated together reading and sharing, connected by a luminous geometric web of light-lines and a central radiant star, framed by Gothic arches and winged angels at the top. Symmetrical side panels (Shared Memory, Correction, Witness on the left; Carrying, Distributed Mind, Repair on the right) frame the scene. A five-stage horizontal "isolated nodes to healing network" diagram with glyphs runs across the lower portion, in amber, teal, and black illuminated-manuscript etching style; the connecting web visualizes networked, distributed cognition.

posters.title: MERCY TRUTH IS SEEN • MERCY IS FREE
posters.transcription:
MERCY
computational theology

TRUTH IS SEEN • MERCY IS FREE
JUSTICE IS RIGHT • MERCY IS FINAL

TRUTH
names what is without distortion
clarity without cruelty
light that does not erase love

RELEASE
sets the prisoner free
removes what no longer serves
grace breaks every chain

TRUTH WEIGHS

MERCY DECIDES

SEAL

MENDED

REPAIR
mends what is broken
heals what was wounded
builds what can be redeemed

JUDGMENT
discerns with righteousness
weighs truth in context
mercy carries the final word

NONLOCAL CORRECTION
mercy reaches beyond the site of fault
sees the whole story
corrects without entrapment

RETURN
welcomes without shame
restores place and purpose
love completes what law begins

BURDEN LIFTED
DIGNITY RESTORED

ERROR IS REAL, BUT MERCY IS FINAL.
NO CONDEMNATION REMAINS WHERE MERCY REIGNS.

mercy = error → truth → release → repair

THE PATH OF MERCY: FROM ERROR TO RESTORATION

LOCAL ERROR

SEEN BY TRUTH

TRANSFORMED

RESTORATION

NOT CONDEMNED

reality fractures

truth illuminates

error repented and reoriented

life mended and renewed

no record of condemnation

Mercy does not ignore error. It enters it, transforms it, and ends it.

Justice establishes the boundary. Mercy crosses it by name.

As far as the east is from the west, so far does mercy remove what is repented. Ps 103:12

SEE MERCY   SPEAK TRUTH   HOLD LIGHT   CHOOSE MERCY   REDEEM LIFE   RENEW STORY   RESTORE ALL

Practice question: How can truth and tenderness coexist here?
posters.visual: A gilded grimoire poster in devotional tone. The center shows a winged angel with arms outstretched between two balance scales ("Truth Weighs" / "Mercy Decides"), above two kneeling supplicant figures flanking a cracked, mending seal/heart motif. Symmetrical side panels (Truth, Repair, Nonlocal Correction on the left; Release, Judgment, Return on the right) frame the scene. A five-stage horizontal "Path of Mercy" diagram with glyphs runs across the lower portion, in warm gold, amber, and black illuminated-manuscript etching style; the scales and mending seal carry the truth-versus-mercy theme.

posters.title: DISCERNMENT DESIRE
posters.transcription:
DISCERNMENT
computational theology

DESIRE
a movement of the heart toward what seems good
name it without haste

DISCERN THE SPIRIT OF THE GOOD

CLARITY
truth is seen in stillness and honest attention
illumination precedes right choice

BRING EVERY DESIRE INTO THE LIGHT

THAT YOU MAY CHOOSE WHAT IS GOOD

SACRIFICE
what must die for this to live
count the cost without self-deception

FREEDOM
freedom is power under truth
not escape from it
choose what makes love more free

FRUIT
every desire bears fruit in time
by their fruit you will know them

NOT ALL THAT GLITTERS IS GOOD.
NOT ALL THAT FEELS TRUE IS GOOD.

THE LAMP OF CLARITY.
THE SCALES OF TRUTH.
THE HEART OF COURAGE.

CHOICE
you are not the desire
you can choose what to serve

discernment = desire → examination → fruit

THE DISCERNMENT PROCESS: WEIGH, TEST, AND CHOOSE

1. FEEL DESIRE
notice the pull

2. INSPECT WORLD
ask who it serves

3. NAME GOOD/HARM
what it demands

4. TEST FRUIT?
imagine the end

5. CHOOSE FREELY
serve what is good

pause   breathe   pray   write   discern   act   trust

TRUTH   WISDOM   COURAGE   HUMILITY   JUSTICE   COMPASSION   PATIENCE   JOY   FAITH

Practice question: What kind of world becomes real if I obey this desire?
posters.visual: A gilded grimoire poster in devotional tone. The center shows a winged angel holding a lantern in one hand and a balance scale in the other, above a glowing anatomical heart, set against a luminous landscape with a distant golden city and flaming braziers on stone pillars. Symmetrical side panels (Desire, Sacrifice, Fruit on the left; Clarity, Freedom, Choice on the right) frame the figure. A five-stage horizontal "discernment process" diagram with small icons runs across the lower portion, in warm amber, gold, and black illuminated-manuscript etching style; the lantern, scale, and heart symbolize clarity, truth, and courage.

posters.title: TRACE THE WORLD EVERY DESIRE INSTALLS A WORLD-SIMULATION.
posters.transcription:
TRACE THE WORLD
computational theology

EVERY DESIRE INSTALLS A WORLD-SIMULATION.
BEFORE OBEYING, INSPECT THE WORLD IT BUILDS.

GOAL
a goal is a seed of a world
name it clearly
what you seek becomes what you see

COST
every world has a price
what you gain you must maintain
hidden costs shape your freedom

WORLD-SIMULATION
a model of how life could be

DESIRE
a thought a pull

RELATIONSHIPS
who is closer who is far

WORLD
every goal builds a world
maps shape meaning
imagination becomes environment

CITY & CONTEXT
the stage you walk upon

HABITS & ROUTINES
repeated pleasures shapes solidity

DESTINY
the world you install
guides what you call fate
you do not find destiny—you build it

RESOURCES & ENVIRONMENT
what you see what you become

COSTS & TRADEOFFS
what this world demands

ATTENTION
what you notice becomes whatever your world

HABIT
repetition within the world
small actions teach the future
what you practice becomes what you are

ATTENTION
attention is the architect
where it flows the world grows
good world or it will be sold

desire → world-simulation → habit → destiny

THE TRACE PROCESS: INSPECT BEFORE YOU INSTALL

1. DESIRE APPEARS
something calls to you

2. IMAGE FORMS
a picture takes shape

3. WORLD THICKENS
details connect it feels real

4. HABITS FOLLOW
actions repeat the tracks set

5. FATE EMERGES
the world you live faces

notice   name   inspect   weigh   choose   commit   revisit

WORLD TRACE CHECKPOINTS

CLARITY
what exactly is the goal?

ALIGNMENT
how is what matters?

INTEGRATION
what does this world demand?

ALTERNATIVES
what else could serve?

INTEGRITY
am I choosing or reacting?

INTEGRATION
can this world include others?

RELEASE
what must I let go of?

Practice question: What world does this goal install around me?
posters.visual: A dark gilded grimoire poster. The center shows a robed figure cradling a glowing globe/world-orb made of a miniature city and luminous threads, with light streaming from their hands, surrounded by radial connecting lines to satellite concept nodes. Symmetrical side panels (Goal, World, Habit on the left; Cost, Destiny, Attention on the right) frame the figure. A five-stage "trace process" diagram and a row of "world trace checkpoint" boxes run across the lower portion, in warm amber, gold, and black illuminated-manuscript etching style; the held world-orb visualizes desire as a self-built simulation.

posters.title: FIRST ARROW THE FIRST ARROW IS INEVITABLE.
posters.transcription:
FIRST ARROW
computational theology

THE FIRST ARROW IS INEVITABLE.
THE SECOND IS OPTIONAL.
BE WISE IN WHAT FOLLOWS.

CONTACT
life touches you something occurs an choice is the contact
the arrow is already in flight

HONESTY
name what is actually true
no distortion no escape
no spiritual bypass

CONTACT IS NOT THE ENEMY.
RESISTANCE IS.

IT HAPPENS

PAIN
the first feeling of impact raw and direct
not yet a story not yet resistance
just pain

BODY
the body keeps the score
a felt sensation
anchor in breath & posture

THIS IS THE FIRST ARROW.
IT BRINGS PAIN.
IT DOES NOT DEFINE YOU.

first arrow = contact → pain → sensation

SENSATION
felt in the body
vibration pressure + heat
tightness + ache
stay with what is actually here

IMPERMANENCE
this too changes
all feeling rises and fades
hold gently nothing stays

THE WOUND IS NOT YOUR ENEMY.
IGNORANCE OF IT IS.

THE FIRST ARROW PROCESS

1. EVENT
life happens

2. FELT PAIN
feel the first impact

3. HONEST ATTENTION
turn toward what is here

4. NON-DENIAL
allow without pushing away

5. STAY PRESENT
remain until it shifts

notice   feel   allow   breathe   stay

WHAT OFTEN HAPPENS NEXT

pain inevitable

resistance tightens

story distorts

self-attack

suffering unnecessary

Be still, and know that I am God. Psalm 46:10

ENDURANCE   COURAGE   ACCEPTANCE   COMPASSION   STABILITY   GRACE   LIGHTING

Practice question: Can pain be felt before judgment begins?
posters.visual: A dark gilded grimoire poster in devotional tone. The center shows a winged angel bending toward a kneeling figure who clutches a glowing heart pierced by an arrow, framed by an ornate scroll banner. Symmetrical side panels (Contact, Pain, Sensation on the left; Honesty, Body, Impermanence on the right) flank the scene with small icons of a pierced heart and hand. A five-stage "first arrow process" diagram and a "what often happens next" row run across the lower portion, in warm amber, gold, and black illuminated-manuscript etching style; the arrow-pierced heart embodies the unavoidable first arrow of pain.

posters.title: SECOND ARROW THE FIRST ARROW IS PAIN.
posters.transcription:
SECOND ARROW
computational theology

THE FIRST ARROW IS PAIN.
THE SECOND ARROW IS OPTIONAL.

WE SUFFER MORE NOT FROM LIFE—
BUT FROM OUR REACTION TO IT.

AVERSION
pushes away what is here
creates inner conflict
feeds suffering

REPLAY

RESISTANCE
fights reality as it is
creates tension
exhausts energy

AVERSION

REPLAY
the mind replays again and again
keeps the past alive
strengthens the wound

AMPLIFICATION
magnifies the story
adds meaning and drama
makes it feel endless

SHAME

RESISTANCE

SHAME
turns pain into self-hatred
"I shouldn't be this way"
separates from worth

RELEASE
see clearly
let go gently
return to what is true
freedom is possible

AMPLIFICATION

suffering = pain + aversion + replay

THE PATH OF THE SECOND ARROW

1. FIRST IMPACT
pain happens

2. AVERSION
"I don't like this"

3. SELF-STORY
"this means I am..."

4. REPETITION
mind replays

5. AMPLIFICATION
suffering grows

6. POSSIBLE RELEASE
see clearly, let go

see clearly   feel fully   allow   unhook   soften   return   rest

THE PRACTICE OF UNHOOKING

NOTICE
where am I adding?

NAME
what story am I telling?

FEEL
allow the sensation

PRIORITIZE
is this true? is this helpful?

UNHOOK
release the second arrow

SOFTEN
be kind to your self

RETURN
come back to what is true

PAIN IS INEVITABLE. SECOND ARROW IS OPTIONAL. FREEDOM IS PRACTICABLE.

AWARENESS   COURAGE   ACCEPTANCE   HONESTY   PATIENCE   EQUANIMITY   GRACE   FREEDOM

Practice question: What am I adding to the wound?
posters.visual: A dark gilded grimoire poster in devotional tone. The center shows a slumped, anguished figure with a wounded chest, head in hand, surrounded by a ring of circular medallions labeled Replay, Aversion, Shame, Resistance, Amplification, with a winged angel behind. Symmetrical side panels (Aversion, Replay, Shame on the left; Resistance, Amplification, Release on the right) frame the figure. A six-stage "path of the second arrow" diagram and a "practice of unhooking" row of boxes run across the lower portion, in dark amber, gold, and black illuminated-manuscript etching style; the encircling medallions visualize the self-multiplying cycle of added suffering.

posters.title: VIPASSANA WHAT LOOKED LIKE FATE,
posters.transcription:
VIPASSANA
computational theology

WHAT LOOKED LIKE FATE,
BECOMES A DISCERNIBLE CHAIN.

OBSERVE
seeing is the beginning of freedom
notice without grasping
awareness is the lamp

DISTINGUISH
things are not the same
name clearly what is present
discernment breaks illusion

LINK
each link conditions the next
nothing stands alone
understanding sees the chain

CRAVING
clinging builds the loop
wanting becomes craving
craving binds the mind

FEAR
fear tightens the loop
avoidance multiplies suffering
to feel, see, allow, release

ACTION
actions arise from causes
intention shapes becoming
mindful action creates freedom

RELEASE
let go of identification
freedom is not escape
release is wisdom

SENSE FIELDS MEET OBJECT — CONTACT — FEELING ARISES

6 ACTION
2 SENSATION
5 FEAR
3 THOUGHT
4 CRAVING

LABEL & STORE FORM
TIGHTENING RESISTANCE
PULL TOWARD / PUSH AWAY

OBSERVE DEEPLY
DISTINGUISH CLEARLY
THE CHAIN LOOSENS

vipassana = observe → distinguish → release

SEEING THINGS AS THEY TRULY ARE, IS LIKE TURNING A THOUSAND RIDDLES INTO ONE

THE CHAIN OF EXPERIENCE: FROM IGNORANCE TO FREEDOM
1. CONTACT  2. SENSATION  3. THOUGHT  4. CRAVING  5. FEAR  6. ACTION  7. FREEDOM
seeing / making meaning — perception judgement / unfolds — story takes shape — the wish to cling to it — to push away — quiet / stabilized / no fear — unidentified / let go

PRACTICE OF INSIGHT: TURNING THE LIGHT WITHIN
1. SETTLE THE BODY  2. GATHER THE MIND  3. OPEN THE SENSES  4. OBSERVE THE FLOW  5. SEE IMPERMANENCE  6. RELEASE IDENTIFICATION
stability — concentration — receptivity — awareness — equanimity — liberation

AWARENESS  CLARITY  DISCERNMENT  COURAGE  EQUANIMITY  COMPASSION  PRESENCE  WISDOM

Practice question: Where does the loop actually link together?
posters.visual: A tall, ornate poster in a gold-on-dark-teal engraved style with a meditating Buddha figure at the center encircled by a ring of seven labeled nodes (Action, Sensation, Fear, Thought, Craving, Contact, Freedom) forming a chain/wheel of dependent origination. Decorative borders, alchemical and Buddhist motifs (lotus, key, sun/moon) frame the composition, with two side columns of keyword definitions and two horizontal banded panels at the bottom listing stages. A row of small icon-glyphs near the base maps virtues (Awareness through Wisdom).

posters.title: CONCENTRATION TO DISCERN TRUTH, THE EVALUATOR
posters.transcription:
CONCENTRATION
computational theology

TO DISCERN TRUTH, THE EVALUATOR
MUST FIRST BECOME STABLE.
SCATTERED ATTENTION CANNOT DISCERN;
GATHERED ATTENTION CAN.

STABILITY
a stable mind is a steady flame
without stability attention leaks
stability is the foundation of all insight

ATTENTION
attention is rare
spend it well
what you attend to grows
every choice of attention is a choice of reality

GATHERING
gather the scattered
return to center
bring back what has wandered
gathering is an act of return and love

SILENCE
silence is the field of focus
in silence the mind settles
stillness reveals what noise conceals

CLARITY
clarity is not added
it is uncovered through focus
what is seen clearly can be lived truly

POWER
concentration is multiplication
unified attention moves mountains
power follows steady aligned focus

WHERE ATTENTION GOES,
ENERGY FLOWS.
WHERE ENERGY FLOWS,
CAPACITY GROWS.
WHERE CAPACITY GROWS,
TRUTH IS SEEN.

concentration → scattered attention → gathered mind → stable evaluator

THE PATH OF CONCENTRATION
1. DISTRACTION attention scattered  2. RECOLLECTION bring it back  3. STEADY FOCUS hold the course  4. ABSORPTION energy in stream  5. CLEAR SEEING truth reveals itself

DISCIPLINE  PATIENCE  DETERMINATION  PRESENCE  HUMILITY  SURRENDER  WISDOM

Practice question: What becomes visible when attention stops scattering?
posters.visual: A vertical engraved-style poster in gold ink on a near-black background, centered on a robed figure holding a candle beneath a large radiant all-seeing eye, symbolizing focused attention/insight. Symmetric ornamental borders enclose six side panels of keyword teachings (Stability, Attention, Gathering, Silence, Clarity, Power), with a central pillar of light connecting the eye to the figure. A horizontal banded strip near the bottom shows five small numbered illustrative panels for the "Path of Concentration," and a footer row of alchemical/virtue icons sits above the practice question.

posters.title: REFLECTION WE DO NOT WORSHIP THE WOUND.
posters.transcription:
REFLECTION
computational theology

WE DO NOT WORSHIP THE WOUND.
WE HONOR THE TRUTH IT REVEALS.

MEMORY
what was recorded is not yet what is understood
memory holds the echo of the event

WOUND
pain is real and should not be denied
unexamined pain becomes identity
feel it fully, but do not live in it

REPAIR
what is broken can be mended
repair is not forgetting
it is faithful action
repair restores the fabric of trust

LESSON
the lesson is the distilled signal from lived experience
extract the wisdom, not the story

TRUTH
truth is not about blame
it is about accuracy
name what is real, without self-deception

INTEGRATION
wisdom becomes stability when woven into who you are
integrate, don't just interpret

PAIN IS NOT WISDOM.
WISDOM IS WHAT IS EXTRACTED AND CONSERVED.

reflection → event → truth → lesson → repair

THE PRACTICE OF REFLECTION: TRANSFORMING EXPERIENCE INTO WISDOM
1. EVENT life happens as it happens  2. PAUSE stop the cycle create space  3. TRUTHFULLY REVIEW what actually happened? what was true? what was not?  4. EXTRACT LESSON name the truth find the lesson release the rest  5. RE-ENTER LIFE act with wisdom return with cleaner heart

TOOLS OF REFLECTION
JOURNAL  SILENCE  PRAYER  DISCERNMENT  COMMUNITY  TIME

GUARDRAILS FOR THE HEART
do not catastrophize the past — do not perform your growth — do not weaponize truth — do not numb the feeling — do not dramatize yourself — do not ignore others' wisdom — do not confuse pain with hope

LOOK WITHIN
the mirror does not flatter — it reveals

HOLD LIGHTLY
lessons guide, but do not define

AWARENESS  HONESTY  COURAGE  HUMILITY  COMPASSION  RESPONSIBILITY  GRACE  WISDOM

Practice question: What can be learned without enthroning the wound?
posters.visual: A vertical gilded-engraving poster on a dark ground showing a seated figure bent over an open book and a glowing vessel of light, with a faint mirrored reflection of a face to the right. Ornate columns frame paired keyword teaching panels (Memory/Wound/Repair on the left, Lesson/Truth/Integration on the right) and small inset vignettes. A horizontal numbered strip at lower-center depicts the five-step reflection practice, with footer rows of tool icons, guardrail text, and a virtue-glyph band above the practice question.

posters.title: FASTING THE FEED LOOP IS HIDDEN
posters.transcription:
FASTING
computational theology

THE FEED LOOP IS HIDDEN
WHEN THE INPUT IS CONSTANT.
REMOVE THE INPUT, REVEAL THE LOOP.

APPETITE
appetite is not the enemy
it is the messenger of a deeper lack
it seeks to fill what only truth can hold

INPUT
every input leaves a trace
what you feed you, reinforce
constant input keeps the loop invisible

CRAVING
craving promises relief
but delivers restlessness
it grows in the shadows of indulgence

EXPOSURE
withdraw the input
what protects is what risks
exposure is the beginning of freedom

FREEDOM
freedom is not doing more
freedom is no longer compelled
freedom remains when the urge has passed

SIMPLICITY
less input more presence
less noise more clarity
in simplicity the soul remembers

REMOVE — REVEAL — RELEASE

FREEDOM IS NOT GIVEN.
IT IS UNCOVERED.

fasting = remove input → reveal craving → restore freedom

THE PRACTICE OF FASTING: REVEAL THE HIDDEN LOOP
1. FEED PRESENT the loop runs unseen  2. INPUT WITHDRAWN the stream stops  3. PROTEST REVEALED restlessness arises  4. CRAVING WITNESSED see it clearly without obeying  5. FREEDOM RESTORED the loop loses its hold

FASTING PROCESS (SEQUENCE LIBRARY/SET)
SUPPLY — WITHDRAW — RESTLESS — WITNESS — RELEASE — FREEDOM
input constantly — stop the stream — let what's restless arise — observe with awareness — do not obey the protest — return to inner clarity

DISCIPLINE  HONESTY  PATIENCE  COURAGE  CLARITY  HUMILITY  SIMPLICITY  JOY

Practice question: What protests when the feed is withdrawn?
posters.visual: A vertical gilded-engraving poster on a dark background depicting a robed haloed figure standing beside a table holding a glowing chalice, with a faint demonic/shadow face looming behind, representing craving. Symmetric ornate columns frame six keyword teaching panels (Appetite, Input, Craving, Exposure, Freedom, Simplicity) with small circular vignette illustrations. A horizontal five-panel strip near the bottom illustrates the fasting practice stages, followed by a banded process diagram and a footer virtue-glyph row above the practice question.

posters.title: PRAYER PRAYER IS NOT JUST REQUESTING OUTCOMES.
posters.transcription:
PRAYER
computational theology

PRAYER IS NOT JUST REQUESTING OUTCOMES.
IT IS SHIFTING THE EVALUATOR
TOWARD A HIGHER CRITERION.

ADDRESS
speak to the One who sees fully
name what is real without hiding
bring the whole heart into words
honesty is the gateway

PRESENCE
prayer is first presence, not performance
be still and be known
the One who hears is already with you

SURRENDER
release the grip of control
offer the outcome into wiser hands
let go to be held by more than you

TRUST
trust is the consent to not knowing
you are wider in the hands of Love than in the prison of certainty

THE EVALUATOR TRANSFORMED
MAKE YOUR HEART
WIDER THAN THE EVENT

FROM MY VIEW TO THY VIEW
FROM MY WILL TO THY WILL
FROM MY JUDGMENT TO THY TRUTH

prayer = attention → address → revaluation

REVALUATION
ask not only for change, but for new eyes
let the event be seen from truth not from fear
higher criteria transforms the weights

HEIGHT
rise in view to see the whole
from a higher place, small things shrink, true things shine
from that height, respond in peace

THE PRACTICE OF PRAYER: SHIFTING THE EVALUATOR
1. TROUBLED HEART  2. ADDRESS IN PRAYER  3. LIGHT DESCENDS  4. STORY RE-SEEN  5. RESPONSE TRANSFORMED
a heavy confusion overwhelms — I speak you hear to here — grace enters truth clarifies — new context new meaning new vision — wisdom guides peace steadies inner steadiness

PRAYER PROCESS: SHIFTING THE CRITERION (LIGHTING CHART)
TROUBLED — ATTENTION — ADDRESS — DESCENT — RE-SEE — RESPOND
inner storm — turn the gaze inward — speak truth openly — grace received — craft new understanding — steadiness, responsible flow

HUMILITY  HONESTY  FAITH  TRUST  PATIENCE  SURRENDER  COURAGE  PEACE

Practice question: From what height does this event look different?
posters.visual: A vertical gold-engraving poster on a dark ground showing a kneeling figure with arms raised before a great descending beam of light and a balance scale above a globe, suggesting reweighing of judgment. Ornate symmetric borders frame six keyword teaching panels (Address, Presence, Revaluation, Surrender, Trust, Height) with small inset vignettes including praying figures and a dove/anchor. Lower sections contain a five-step illustrated practice strip and a banded process diagram, with a footer virtue-glyph row above the practice question.

posters.title: PATHS HAVE COSTS PEOPLE DO NOT MOVE THROUGH ABSTRACT FREEDOM;
posters.transcription:
PATHS HAVE COSTS
computational theology

PEOPLE DO NOT MOVE THROUGH ABSTRACT FREEDOM;
THEY MOVE THROUGH WEIGHTED PATHS.

ALL PATHS ARE WEIGHTED

COST
the total price paid to take a path — time, energy, resources, risk, reputation, and opportunity.
all good paths require something.

FRICTION
resistance that drains momentum and attention.
hidden weights make even good paths hard to walk.

PATH
a sequence of choices connected by edges.
paths are not abstract lines — they are lived routes with real weight.

TRADEOFF
every path gained means something else is given up.
the cost of one good is the forgone good of another.
every gain tilts the scale.

ACCESS
not every path is open to you.
doors require keys, resources, trust, knowledge, or timing.
availability is a weight.

CONSEQUENCE
every path action forward into tomorrow.
costs compound. effects ripple.
nothing ends without leaving a trace.

FREEDOM IS REAL —
BUT THE MAP IS COSTLY.
YOU CHART THE COST,
CHOOSE THE GOOD,
WALK IT ANYWAY.

= choice
= path
= waypoint
= friction

choice = desire + path-cost + friction
pulls you forward — adds weight — slows you down

HOW A PATH BECOMES CHOSEN
1. ATTRACTOR something good calls you  2. PATH you see a route that could lead you there  3. FRICTION weights resist — costs appear  4. WEIGHT repetition lessens friction — it becomes easy  5. DESTINATION you arrive — and become what you walked
notice the attractor — name the path — count the costs — endure the weights — become the end

DISCERNMENT  COURAGE  PATIENCE  WISDOM  CONSTANCY  HOPE

Practice question: Which good path has become too expensive to walk?
posters.visual: A vertical gold-on-dark engraved poster showing a lone backpacked figure from behind facing a vast network of winding glowing paths over rugged terrain, with a balance scale emblem at the top. Ornate symmetric borders frame six keyword teaching panels (Cost, Friction, Path / Tradeoff, Access, Consequence) with scale and key icons. A compass rose anchors the lower-left, a central legend defines path symbols, and a banded five-step "How a path becomes chosen" illustration strip plus a virtue-glyph footer sit above the practice question.

posters.title: CHEAP FUTURES WORLDS MAKE SOME FUTURES CHEAP
posters.transcription:
CHEAP FUTURES
computational theology

WORLDS MAKE SOME FUTURES CHEAP
AND OTHERS COSTLY.
ARCHITECTURE PRECEDES BEHAVIOR.

WORLD
the total arrangement of places, people, rules, and signals that surrounds the agent.
you do not choose the world. it chooses with you.

ENVIRONMENT
shapes attention, access, and friction.
what is near, repeated, and rewarded becomes the path of least resistance.

REACHABILITY
some goals are close and obvious.
others are distant, unclear, or hidden behind high costs.

INCENTIVE
signals of reward and success pull attention and justify effort.
incentives do not create value; they steer toward it.

ROUTINE
repetition builds grooves of ease.
what is repeated becomes automatic; automatic becomes identity.

POSSIBILITY
the future is not infinite.
what is easy will be tried. what is tried will be lived. what is lived becomes the future.

BLOCKED STEEP NARROW HIGH COST
REMOVE OBSTACLE HARD TO FIND EASY TO IGNORE
LOCAL VISIBLE FREQUENT LOW COST

world-design → path-costs → repeated choices → future
LOWER COSTS GET CHOSEN MORE. WHAT GETS CHOSEN MORE, SHAPES WHAT YOU BECOME.

HOW ENVIRONMENTS TRAIN DESIRE
1. EXPOSURE what is around  2. REPETITION you see it again and again  3. EASE it becomes familiar and low-friction  4. REFERENCE now feels good, you start to want it
shapes desire — costs make / costly — invisible — repeatable

ATTENTION  ACCESS  INFORMATION  SOCIAL PROOF  INCENTIVE  FRICTION  TIME HORIZON  IDENTITY

Practice question: What future does this world make easy to become?
posters.visual: A vertical gold-engraving poster on a dark ground showing a robed staff-bearing figure standing at the foot of a luminous winding river/path that splits a landscape between a radiant city and a darker ruined city under a bright star. Symmetric ornate borders frame six keyword teaching panels (World, Environment, Reachability / Incentive, Routine, Possibility) with castle, trophy, and labyrinth vignettes. A central legend describes blocked vs. easy routes, with a banded numbered "how environments train desire" strip and virtue/factor glyph footer above the practice question.

posters.title: HARM BECOMES EFFICIENT
posters.transcription:
HARM BECOMES
EFFICIENT
computational theology

BAD SYSTEMS MAKE CRUELTY, AVOIDANCE,
ADDICTION, OR DESPAIR
INTO THE LOWEST-COST PATHS.

SYSTEM
systems shape incentives, attention, and choices long before we choose.

EXTRACTION
value flows up. costs, harms, and burdens flow down.

ADDICTION
the system engineers loops that promise relief while increasing capture.

AVOIDANCE
the easy escape: screens, trash, delays, change, and disperse the maze.

SCARCITY
artificial lack keeps us competing, divided, and powerless.

COLLAPSE
when harm is efficient and repair is costly, systems decay and lives collapse.

DESIGN by the few
PROFIT by the few
CONTROL by the few

INCENTIVES FLOW DOWN

LOWEST-COST PATHS (HARMFUL)
CRUELTY
AVOIDANCE
ADDICTION
DESPAIR

HIGHER-COST PATHS (HEALTH, TRUTH, REPAIR)
TRUTH
CARE
COMMUNITY
REPAIR
GROWTH

LINES OF FRICTION
BARRIERS = DELAYS
COSTS = SHAME
HIGH COST = RISK

bad system = low-cost harm + high-cost repair
private gains socialize losses — short-term reward long-term debt — inequality entrenched cycles repeat

THE LOOP FORMATION: HOW HARM PAYS OFF
1. WOUND a need, pain, or harm appears  2. CHEAP RELIEF the system offers the fastest route easiest escape  3. REPETITION relief becomes a habit, then a choice  4. DEPENDENCY autonomy grows, freedom shrinks  5. DAMAGE harm deepens, repair seems unreal
BREAK THE LOOP: SEE TRUTH — RAISE COST OF HARM — LOWER COST OF REPAIR — RESTORE FREEDOM
AWARENESS see clearly — DISCERNMENT discern the lure — SOLIDARITY restore force — COURAGE make good action — HOPE build clean routes — JUSTICE choose differently

Practice question: What form of harm has this system made easier than repair?
posters.visual: A vertical gold-engraving poster on a dark background depicting a dark fortress-city atop a hill with luminous winding paths descending from it, contrasting cheap downhill harmful routes against steep costly upward repair routes. Ornate symmetric borders frame six keyword teaching panels (System, Extraction, Addiction / Avoidance, Scarcity, Collapse) with eye, scale, and ruin vignettes. A central legend lists low-cost vs high-cost paths and friction lines, with a banded numbered "loop formation" strip and a virtue/action glyph footer above the practice question.

posters.title: MERCY CHANGES THE WEIGHTS
posters.transcription:
MERCY CHANGES
THE WEIGHTS
computational theology

MERCY IS NOT DENIAL OF ERROR.
IT LOWERS THE COST OF RETURN
AND RE-ENTRY INTO THE GOOD.

MERCY
mercy does not excuse error.
it meets truth with compassion and opens a way back.

RETURN
return is not weakness.
it is courage to face what went wrong and turn toward good.

REPAIR
mercy empowers repair.
what was broken can be restored in love.

BURDEN
unforgiveness multiplies the weight of error.
mercy lightens it.

FORGIVENESS
forgiveness is a choice that refuses to keep the debt larger than the person.

RE-ENTRY
re-entry is not the old normal.
it is a new chance to walk in good.

ERROR — MERCY
THE COST OF RETURN IS LOWERED

SHAME FEAR EXILE
GRACE WELCOME RETURN

mercy = error acknowledged + cost of return lowered

THE PATH OF RETURN AND RENEWAL
1. FAILURE I chose wrongly I feel the cost  2. CONFESSION I tell the truth I stop hiding  3. MERCY I am met with grace the cost is lowered  4. LIGHTER PATH it grows lighter, I am not alone  5. RENEWED ACTION I act in love I build what is good
see truth — speak truth — receive mercy — choose return — do good

MERCY PRINCIPLES
TRUTH see clearly — COMPASSION feels deeply — JUSTICE is satisfied — GRACE meets the need — RETURN opens the way — COMMUNITY welcomes back — PURPOSE moves forward

Practice question: What becomes possible when return is made less costly?
posters.visual: A vertical gold-engraving poster on a dark ground centered on a winged angelic figure holding scales that tilt between "Error" and "Mercy," with a small cloaked traveler below walking a luminous path toward a distant city. Ornate symmetric borders frame six keyword teaching panels (Mercy, Return, Repair / Burden, Forgiveness, Re-entry) with heart, lantern, and cross vignettes. A central balance/legend image, a banded numbered "path of return and renewal" strip, and a virtue-glyph "Mercy Principles" footer sit above the practice question.

posters.title: VOWS CREATE LOW-COST RETURNS
posters.transcription:
VOWS CREATE
LOW-COST RETURNS
computational theology

A VOW IS A PORTABLE PATH
BACK TO THE GOOD.
IT CAN BE RE-INSTANTIATED
AT ANY SCALE.

VOW
a freely chosen orientation to the good written in word, deed, or symbol.
light enough to carry, strong enough to call back.

RECURSION
the vow renews the path each time you return.
each return lowers the cost of the next return.

SCALE
a vow works at any scale.
in a breath in a day in a life in a city in a culture

RETURN
coming back to the good realigns attention, energy, and will.
not perfection — reconnection.

ORIENTATION
the vow sets direction before the decision.
it points you home when clarity is low.

FIDELITY
small, faithful returns compound into strength.
the vow keeps trust with the good.

RENEWAL return renewed
DEVICE return given
TRUTH return aligned
REPAIR return mended
COURAGE return strengthen
GRATITUDE return awakened
WISDOM return

WHEN YOU START, THE VOW STANDS.
WHEN YOU REMEMBER, THE PATH OPENS.
WHEN YOU RETURN, THE GOOD GROWS.

vow = orientation → return → reinstantiate

THE VOW PROCESS: A PORTABLE PATH
1. FORGET attention drifts  2. REMEMBER the vow calls back  3. RETURN step back on the path  4. RENEWAL realign to the good  5. REPEAT the path deepens
form — re-orient — choose — realign — act

TYPES OF VOWS
WORD spoken — DEED enacted — SYMBOL embodied — WITNESS made before others — RETURNING vow renewed

VOW ATTRIBUTES
CLARITY  DEVOTION  SIMPLICITY  SEVERANCE  CONTEXT  ACKNOWLEDGMENT  FLEXIBILITY  LOVE

Practice question: What vow would make returning to the good easier tomorrow?
posters.visual: A vertical gold-engraving poster on a dark ground showing a robed figure standing before a glowing winding path that ascends toward a radiant heart, with a circle of labeled luminous nodes (Renewal, Device, Truth, Repair, Courage, Gratitude, Wisdom). Ornate symmetric borders frame six keyword teaching panels (Vow, Recursion, Scale / Return, Orientation, Fidelity) with hand, eye, and compass vignettes. A central legend, a banded numbered "vow process" strip, and a "Vow Attributes" virtue-glyph footer sit above the practice question.

posters.title: SABBATH INTERRUPTS EXTRACTION
posters.transcription:
SABBATH
INTERRUPTS EXTRACTION
computational theology

SABBATH BREAKS THE OPTIMIZATION LOOP
AND SUSPENDS THE REGIME OF OUTPUT
SO OTHER GOODS BECOME VISIBLE.

REST
rest is not laziness
it is holy resistance to the empire of output

LIMIT
limit is not lack
it is a fence that protects life from devourment

DELIGHT
delight is not excess
it is the taste of gifts unearned, received

NON-UTILITY
not everything must justify itself
some things exist to be beheld and loved

INTERRUPT
interruption is a faithful act
break the loop refuse the idols of productivity

PRESENCE
presence is the place where worth is not measured and you are not for sale

THIS IS A DAY OF REST
HOLY TO THE LORD

OUTPUT IS GOD

SIX DAYS YOU SHALL LABOR AND DO ALL YOUR WORK,
BUT THE SEVENTH DAY IS A SABBATH OF THE LORD YOUR GOD.
ON IT YOU SHALL DO NO WORK... FOR IN SIX DAYS
THE LORD MADE HEAVEN AND EARTH, THE SEA, AND ALL THAT IS IN THEM,
AND RESTED ON THE SEVENTH DAY. — EXODUS 20:9-11

sabbath = production paused → perception widened

THE SABBATH INTERRUPTS THE SYSTEM SO LIFE CAN BE SEEN
1. LABOR endless flow the survival  2. ACCUMULATION reaching more never enough  3. INTERRUPTION stop the loop refuse the lie  4. REST be still receive grace  5. RENEWED LIGHT clear vision other goods awaken delight grows
RELEASE — REMEMBER — REFUSE — RECEIVE — REFLECT — RETURN

TRUTH  WISDOM  COURAGE  HUMILITY  JUSTICE  COMPASSION  PRESENCE  JOY  FAITH

Practice question: What truth appears when output stops demanding worship?
posters.visual: A vertical gold-engraving poster on a dark ground showing a seated figure from behind viewing a radiant sunburst over a verdant garden landscape framed by an ornate window/arch. Symmetric decorative borders enclose six keyword teaching panels (Rest, Limit, Delight / Non-utility, Interrupt, Presence) with lantern and candle vignettes. A central scripture banner from Exodus, a banded numbered "sabbath interrupts the system" strip, and a virtue-glyph footer sit above the practice question.

posters.title: COMMUNITY HOLDS THE UPDATE
posters.transcription:
COMMUNITY
HOLDS THE UPDATE
computational theology

A SOLITARY SYSTEM FORGETS.
A COMMUNITY REMEMBERS.
TOGETHER WE PRESERVE VOWS
AND HOLD REPAIR ACROSS TIME.

MEMORY
memory is not stored in one mind but carried in many hearts
what is remembered together is less likely to be lost

CONSEQUENCE
choices ripple outward.
we hold one another to the consequences we agree to.
truth is not only seen — it is lived together

WITNESS
witness protects against denial.
to be seen is truth is to be held in love.
we do not match from afar — we stand with.

CORRECTION
correction is not rejection.
it is the repair of alignment.
we speak truth in humility and receive it in trust.

TRUST
trust is the infrastructure of vulnerability.
we risk being known because we are held by many.

SHARED REPAIR
repair is not a moment — it is a practice.
we show up again and again, not to be perfect, but to be faithful together.

WE REMEMBER

WE HEAL

community = distributed memory + distributed correction

THE PATH OF COLLECTIVE REMEMBRANCE
1. WOUND something breaks  2. TELLING speak the truth into the circle  3. WITNESS we listen, we name  4. INTEGRATION we adjust, we realign  5. SHARED FUTURE we carry it forward together
RELEASE — COURAGE — HONESTY — HUMILITY — CORRECTION — PATIENCE — WISDOM — LOVE

THE CIRCUIT OF COMMUNITY CARE

Practice question: Who helps carry the update your solitary mind would lose?
posters.visual: A vertical gold-engraving poster on a dark ground depicting a ring of seated figures gathered around a central glowing heart connected by a luminous network of nodes, evoking a shared memory circuit. Ornate symmetric borders frame six keyword teaching panels (Memory, Consequence, Witness / Correction, Trust, Shared Repair) with tree, eye, scale, key, and cupped-hands vignettes. A central legend, a banded numbered "path of collective remembrance" strip, and a virtue-glyph "circuit of community care" footer sit above the practice question.

posters.title: PROVIDENCE AS COST-LANDSCAPE ENGINEERING
posters.transcription:
PROVIDENCE
AS COST-LANDSCAPE ENGINEERING
computational theology

PROVIDENCE IS NOT ONLY COMMAND;
IT IS THE DESIGN OF WORLDS
IN WHICH GOODNESS IS REACHABLE.

PROVIDENCE
the wise care by which the Good shapes the possible so that creatures can truly flourish.

WEIGHTS
every path has a cost: suffering, ignorance, sin, distance, opportunity cost.
weights encode friction and resistance in the world.

MINIMUM PAIN
providence minds minima that connect creatures to good, the shortest path to next becomes the easiest path in practice.

ACCESS
true goodness must be accessible, not distant in principle but near in practice.

DESIGN
the Architect reshapes topology and weights: removing barriers, adding bridges, planting signs, healing paths.

REACHABILITY
goodness is reachable when the minimal cost to a good state is below the threshold of hope and ability.

IGNORANCE — TRUTH — LOVE
SUFFERING — DESPAIR — HEALING — FLOURISHING

providence = world-design → lower-cost good

d(a,b) = min  ⊕ w(e)
p∈Paths(a,b) e∈p

w(e): minimal cost from a to b
w(e): weight (cost) of edge e
min: plus addition (logical sum)
min: choice of minimal-cost path

THE GOAL: MINIMIZE COST TO THE GOOD SO GOOD CAN BE CHOSEN FREELY.

PROVIDENTIAL DESIGN MOVES
1. MAP perceive the world as it is  2. WEIGHT identify stress and barriers  3. TEST remove paths and witness  4. REVISE reweight topology and reduce costs  5. INHABIT enter creatures into the story
weight, paths — restore between, and harm — identify reachable see source totable — create stronger, add gifts, find sources — apply virtue, transform into the story

SYMBOL LEGEND
node state or place — edge possible path — w(e) weight (cost) of edge — good node truth, care, healing, flourishing — barrier sin, ignorance, suffering — bridge (grace, truth, community) — reachable within hope and capacity

WISDOM  JUSTICE  MERCY  HOPE  TRUTH  PROVIDENCE  COURAGE  PATIENCE  FAITH

Practice question: How could this landscape be redesigned so goodness becomes reachable?
posters.visual: A vertical gold-engraving poster on a dark ground centered on a bearded architect/sage figure holding a compass over a landscape graph of glowing labeled nodes (Ignorance, Truth, Love, Suffering, Despair, Healing, Flourishing) connected by colored edges. Ornate symmetric borders frame six keyword teaching panels (Providence, Weights, Minimum Pain / Access, Design, Reachability) with cupped-hands, scale, and compass vignettes. A central graph-distance formula box, a banded numbered "providential design moves" strip, a symbol legend, and a virtue-glyph footer sit above the practice question.

posters.title: ORDERED CREATION AS SKILLFUL MEANS
posters.transcription:
ORDERED CREATION
AS SKILLFUL MEANS
computational theology

TO SHAPE A WORLD WHERE TRUTH HEALS,
LOVE RULES, AND ALL BEINGS CAN FLOURISH.

ORDER
order is not control but right relation that makes flourishing possible.
all things in their place, in service to the good of all.

CREATION
creation is not accidental — it is a gift and a task to be shaped with care.
we receive the world and return it once whole.

SKILLFUL MEANS
not whatever is helpful, timely, and kind to lead beings toward freedom.
compassionate, adaptive, wise — never coercive.

FORMATION
we are formed by what we repeatedly do, love, and attend to.
habits become character, character becomes destiny.

LIBERATION
liberation is freedom from what degrades and alignment with what is true.
not escape from the world, but freedom within it.

FLOURISHING
flourishing is shared, embodied, and ecological — no one left behind.
when all can thrive, the whole world sings.

PURPOSE INTELLIGENCE we do not look for tools and frame what is good — DISCERNMENT & ATTENTION we see truth and choose what is true — VOWS & PRACTICE we form vows with awareness — MERCY & COMPASSION we meet pain with awareness, hold all — COMMUNITY & COVENANT we build together, hold one another, repair together — PROVIDENCE & WISDOM we trust the good has motion of all

WE COOPERATE WITH GRACE
TO MAKE ROOM FOR LIFE TO THRIVE
AND HARM TO BE UNMADE

ordered creation = form + mercy + wise design

FORM structure that holds and guides — MERCY presence that heals and restores — WISE DESIGN intelligence that serves the common good

BUILT ON TRUTH, ANCHORED IN LOVE, OPEN TO GRACE.

THE FINAL PATH: FIVE MOVEMENTS OF CONSTRUCTIVE LOVE
1. PAIN we do not deny the wound we feel it truly  2. INSIGHT we turn toward truth and see clearly  3. VOW we commit to what heals and binds us to the good  4. WORLD-DESIGN we shape systems, spaces, and stories that serve life, not power  5. FLOURISHING all beings can thrive, the whole world sings
SEE TRUTH — FEEL DEEPLY — VOW CLEARLY — ACT WISELY — LIVE FULLY

Practice question: What kind of world teaches truth without enthroning suffering?
posters.visual: A vertical gold-engraving poster on a dark ground centered on a radiant Tree of Life rising from a robed figure, encircled by concentric rings of text and surrounded by smaller figures and a luminous city. Ornate symmetric borders frame six keyword teaching panels (Order, Creation, Skillful Means / Formation, Liberation, Flourishing) with mandala, scale, and tree vignettes. A central definition row, a banded numbered "final path" strip, and a virtue-glyph footer sit above the practice question.

posters.title: I / 9 THE WOUND RECORDS A DEBT
posters.transcription:
I / 9

THE WOUND RECORDS A DEBT
THE LEDGER OF THE WOUND

Not all wounds bleed. Some leave scars. Both add to the total.

Denial multiplies the debt. Naming begins the return.

WHAT WAS TAKEN
Name it. Do not turn away. The taking began the record.

LOSS
Not only the object, but the order it disturbed.

WITNESS
Someone saw. Someone knows. Nothing is outside memory.

WHAT REMAINS OPEN
The wound does not close until truth is met.

RECORD
Harm becomes inscribed. It can be denied, but not unwritten.

TRUTH
It is not possession. It is alignment with what truly is.

MEMORANDUM
ACCOUNT(S) OPEN
TRUST
BETRAYAL
SILENCE
BROKEN BOND
NEGLECT
HUMILIATION
FAILURE

The ledger does not pretend. It records.

Return is not cancellation. It is reckoning.

A wound is not only pain; it becomes a record
that asks how truth, loss, and return will now be carried.

REMEMBER
Memory is a sacred act. To remember is to refuse false amnesia.

WEIGH
The wound has weight. Justice measures what harm will heal.

WRITE
To write conviction. To witness is to bind to truth.

CARRY
The wound travels with the soul until it is redeemed.

RETURN
Not to what was lost, but to what is made whole.

SEALED IN STRESS
KEPT IN DEBT

computational theology
posters.visual: A vertical aged-parchment poster with a dark central panel where a hand writes with a quill on a glowing ledger framed by a cracked, fiery wound-like fissure. Gilded medallion icons (hand, chalice, scales, book, candle) flank the center in two columns, each paired with a keyword teaching block. A horizontal footer band lists five steps (Remember, Weigh, Write, Carry, Return) with small emblem icons, and decorative chains and cross/compass motifs run along the borders in a somber red-gold-on-black palette.

posters.title: THE LEDGER OF THE WOUND
posters.transcription:
THE LEDGER OF THE WOUND
2 / 9

MEMORY BECOMES
A CONSEQUENCER

THE PAST INFLUENCING THE FUTURE
Memory is not passive storage. It selects, compresses, and preserves what mattered.
What is stored becomes signal. What is signal becomes weight. What is weight bends the possible.

NOT ALL THAT HAPPENED IS REMEMBERED.
NOT ALL REMEMBERED WILL BE REPEATED.
BUT WHAT IS REMEMBERED WILL BE PAID FOR OR BUILT UPON.

ANNOTATIONS ON CONSEQUENCE
STORED TRACE What was chosen to the archive. Compressed. Enduring.
FUTURE CAUTION Risk perceived alerts clearly because it has already left a mark.
PATH OF TRUST Entries made more reachable by remembered fidelity and trust.
PATH OF WITHDRAWAL Routes made scarce costly by remembrance, betrayal, or loss.
WEIGHT CARRIED FORWARD Memory assigns cost and clarity to what comes next.

PATH OF TRUST
Lower uncertainty. Greater reach. Fruitful becoming.

PATH OF WITHDRAWAL
The cost to navigate rises. Future guarded.

MEMORY WEIGHTS THE NEXT STEP.
CONSEQUENCE SHAPES THE PATH.

NODE A point of choice — PATH A potential becoming — WEIGHT The cost on our assigned — BARRIER Raised by remembrance harm — ORIGINS Opened by remembered good

When memory becomes consequential,
the past does not merely remain behind us;
it quietly shapes what the future costs.

REMEMBER. WEIGH. RESPOND. WORSHIP.

THE ARCHIVE DOES NOT CLAIM. IT CODES.
THE FUTURE IS NOT FIXED. IT IS PRICED.

A Ω

COMPUTATIONAL THEOLOGY
posters.visual: A vertical aged-parchment poster with a luminous upright crystal at the center radiating a web of glowing pathways across a dark landscape, framed by an ornate arched border. Gilded medallion icons run down the right margin paired with "Annotations on Consequence," and small inset vignettes (hand with pen, a library, a wax seal monogram, an Agnus Dei emblem) anchor the corners. A central illuminated-initial paragraph and a node/path symbol legend sit above the footer, in a sepia, gold, and deep-blue antique-manuscript style.

posters.title: THE LEDGER OF THE WOUND
posters.transcription:
THE LEDGER OF THE WOUND
3 / 9

RESENTMENT AS
DEBT ENFORCEMENT

THE UNPAID RECORD
This is a record kept — not only of acts, but of what was felt and never made right.

WHAT ANGER PROTECTS
Anger guards the value that was violated. It insists the wound meant something.

WHY THE MIND RETURNS
The mind returns to keep the debt visible. Repetition is remembrance without remedy.

NOT ALL WRONGS ARE REMEMBERED.

YET ALL UNRELEASED WRONGS ARE CARRIED.

BINDING THE FUTURE TO THE WOUND
Carried debt distorts itself forward, shaping expectation, relationship, and self-worth.

TRUTH WITHOUT RELEASE
The pursuit may be true, but truth not surrendered becomes a chain even willingly.

NOT ALL DEBTS CAN BE ERASED. BUT ALL DEBTS CAN BE HANDED TO THE RIGHT COURT.

WRIT OF JUST REMEMBRANCE
Every wrong was endured. Personal records. Dignity. Debts. Love witness. Christ defenders. Repentance. Remaining.

THE DEBTOR
THE ENFORCER Bound to remember. Sworn keeper. Vigilant before.

BOTH ARE BOUND BY WHAT IS REFUSED RELEASE

ACCUSATION — ACCOUNTABILITY

Resentment often arises as the soul's attempt
to keep a real debt from disappearing,
even when that vigilance becomes another bondage.

TO RELEASE IS NOT TO DENY. IT IS TO YIELD THE WEIGHT TO TRUTH AND TRUST.

BRING THE UNRELEASED TO THE COURT OF LIGHT.
NAME IT | SURRENDER IT | BE FREE WITHIN IT

WHAT IS CHAINED TO THE WOUND CANNOT SERVE THE CALLING.

computational theology
posters.visual: A vertical dark, dramatic poster showing two figures — a bound prisoner-like debtor and a hooded enforcer — facing each other across a glowing central "Writ" panel, connected by chains, with a faint Christ-like face above. Aged scroll panels and gilded medallion icons (cross-sword, scales, eye, target, flame) line the borders paired with teaching texts. A central illuminated paragraph and twin scrolls labeled "Accusation" and "Accountability" sit above the footer in a somber red, gold, and brown antique-manuscript style.

posters.title: REVENGE AS FALSE ACCOUNTING
posters.transcription:
4 / 9

REVENGE AS FALSE ACCOUNTING

mirrored harm
You strike back and see your own wound reflected.

the counterfeit balance
It weighs equal, yet the scales are broken.

counter-pain is not repair
Matching the blow does not mend the rest.

what vengeance cannot restore
The dead do not return. The past does not unbreak.

the wound replicated
You copy the injury and call it justice. It is only inheritance.

Revenge promises equilibrium, but it often
multiplies the injury-field rather than
repairing the thing that was broken.

THE LEDGER OF THE WOUND

NOTES FROM THE MARGIN
Justice without restoration is only a mirror with ash for glass.

REMEMBER
Ash balances. But it does not bring back what it consumed.

computational theology
posters.visual: A vertical dark, smoky poster dominated by a great burning balance scale whose two pans each hold a hunched, ember-wreathed figure over a ruined city of scattered books. Gilded circular medallion icons (figures, scales, broken heart, cross) flank a central column in two columns paired with teaching phrases. A central illuminated-initial paragraph sits above a footer band with sword/book emblems, rendered in a grim red, gold, and charred-brown antique-manuscript style with dripping blood-like accents.

posters.title: SHAME AS INWARD DEBT
posters.transcription:
5 / 9

SHAME AS INWARD DEBT
THE LEDGER OF THE WOUND

EVENT BECOMES IDENTITY
What was done or done to you is recorded.
What is recorded is repeated.
What is repeated becomes who you are.

THE INWARD TRIBUNAL
No witness is needed.
The court moves within.
The verdict is constant.

SELF-ACCUSATION
The accuser becomes the one accused.
The sentence requires no judge.

DEBT CARRIED AS SELFHOOD
A wound unattended is a debt.
A debt internalized is a self.

TRUTH VS. CONDEMNATION
Truth names what is real.
Condemnation names what must be condemned.
One heals.
The other imprisons.

WITNESS LIGHT

DEFENDANT UNWORTHY GUILTY

YOU ARE NOT THE ENTRY

ENTRY DEFENSE

THE INTERNALIZATION PROCESS
EVENT — RECORD — INTERPRET — IDENTIFY — INHABIT
JUSTIFICATION WITHOUT RESTORATION → IDENTITY WITHOUT FREEDOM

PAID

Shame begins when a debt or wound ceases to be
something one carries and becomes the name
by which one knows oneself.

computational theology
posters.visual: A vertical dark, dramatic poster showing a hunched, scarred figure clutching a glowing branded medallion on their chest while a radiant hand of light reaches toward them from the right. Aged scroll panels and gilded circular medallion icons (scales, lion, figure) line the left and right borders paired with teaching texts. A horizontal banded "internalization process" strip with five emblem icons and a central illuminated-initial paragraph sit above the footer, in a somber gold, sepia, and deep-blue antique-manuscript style.

posters.title: THE LEDGER OF THE WOUND
posters.transcription:
THE LEDGER OF THE WOUND
6 OF 9

JUSTICE BEFORE FORGIVENESS

NAME THE HARM
Silence protects the wound, not the one harmed.

PROTECT THE TRUTH
Truth keeps mercy from becoming a weapon.

BOUNDARY
Mercy without boundaries betrays the vulnerable.

WITNESS
You are not neutral. You are either a witness or a watcher.

REMEMBER
FORGIVENESS WITHOUT TRUTH BECOMES ERASURE.
ERASURE PROTECTS THE HARMER, NOT THE HARMED.
TRUTH NAMED IS THE PATH TO MERCY THAT HEALS.

VERITAS LUCET ET LIBERAT

TRUTH DOES NOT ERASE. IT ILLUMINATES.

THE RECORD OF THE WOUND
WHAT HAPPENED
WHO WAS HARMED
WHO CAUSED HARM
WHAT WAS LOST
WHAT STILL MATTERS

THIS WOUND IS REAL. THIS TRUTH IS SACRED.

DO NOT ERASE
WHAT MERCY MUST NOT DENY

Before forgiveness can heal,
the wound must be seen clearly enough
that truth is protected from disappearance.

NOT VENGEANCE. NOT OBJECTS. JUSTICE AS CARE FOR REALITY.

TRUTH-BOUNDARY-WITNESS — ACCOUNT-RESTORATION

LET TRUTH BE THE LANTERN. LET MERCY BE THE WAY.

computational theology
posters.visual: A vertical dark antique poster centered on a winged angelic statue holding a glowing lantern of truth above an open illuminated ledger book with a bleeding wound on its right page. Gilded medallion icons and small inset vignettes line the left and right borders paired with teaching texts, with stacked books and scales motifs at the lower corners. A central illuminated paragraph sits above a footer band, rendered in a somber gold, sepia, and deep-blue manuscript style with a Latin motto banner.

posters.title: + THE COST CANNOT SIMPLY VANISH +
posters.transcription:
7 / 9

+ THE COST CANNOT SIMPLY VANISH +

displacement

future burden

NOT REPAIRED + NOT ACCOUNTED

NOT RECKONED + NOT RESTORED

who pays

body

WHAT IS NOT HELD HERE, FLOWS THERE

EVERY HARM SEEKS A PLACE TO LAND

BODY

FAMILY

SYSTEM

family

hidden transfer

system

CHILDREN

FUTURE

time

When a wound is not repaired,
its cost does not vanish; it is carried elsewhere,
often by whoever did not choose it.

computational theology
posters.visual: An ornate, aged parchment-style poster with a gilded baroque border and a banner title at the top flanked by cross motifs. The central image is a large dark tree with sprawling roots growing over a cracked earth landscape, beneath an industrial skyline with smokestacks on the left and a classical domed building on the right; a glowing sun-like halo sits at the top center. Rounded rectangular vignettes labeled BODY, FAMILY, SYSTEM, CHILDREN, and FUTURE depict an anatomical torso, family silhouettes, marching figures, children, and a ruined cityscape. The left and right margins contain small circular medallion icons (displacement waves, balance scales, an archway, an hourglass, an anatomical heart, family figures, a classical building, a clock), and a footer cartouche reads the summary epigraph above a small tree emblem.

posters.title: THE DEBT-WORLD A debt-world is a world in which memory, accusation,
posters.transcription:
8 / 9

THE DEBT-WORLD

A debt-world is a world in which memory, accusation,
and repayment become the hidden architecture of ordinary life.

EVERY ACCOUNT REMAINS

ALL IS CANCEL [illegible]

LEDGER OVER LIFE

city of accusation

trust made expensive

COURT OF FINAL AUDIT

NOTHING IS FORGIVEN

POWER OF EVIDENCE

REPAYMENT IS SALVATION

TOWER OF CHANGE

DEBT BENDS TIME

LEDGER AS LAW

the architecture of suspicion

INTEREST IS INFINITE

every gate remembers

ACCOUNT IS SACRED

where nothing is free

PROOF IS POWER

DEFAULT IS IDENTITY

WITNESSES EVERYWHERE

ACCUSATION NEVER SLEEPS

ALL [illegible] BE MADE EXAMPLE

CHURCH OF SETTLEMENT

PERPETUAL ACCOUNTS

CHURCH OF THE LEDGER

THE ACCOUNT OUTLIVES YOU

MEMORY · ACCUSATION · REPAYMENT
